title: calendar.query (v1) — RPC request
description:
Structured query contract for the calendar-adviser Unix-socket RPC interface. Filed 2026-05-10 with v0.45 §6.8 (Phase 3 task body enrichment) — first of three grounding-source contracts that feed Phase 3's `## Constraints` section.

Transport: Unix socket at `/var/run/ai-calendar-adviser/query.sock`. Caller authenticated via SO_PEERCRED (kernel uid matched against per-agent uid table in registry). `caller_agent_id` in payload is self-asserted for legibility only; authorisation keys off the verified uid.

Returns events from the calendar-adviser's local SQLite cache (synced from Google Calendar every 15min). Cache-stale (>30min) returns are flagged in the `staleness_seconds` field of the response so callers can degrade gracefully — Phase 3 emits `(calendar unavailable)` rather than fabricating per AP-1.

Read-only. `calendar.write_event.v1` (blast radius `external-write`) is deferred to v2 of the agent.

Calendar selection: a `person` argument resolves to a default calendar set per the registry-mapped routing (e.g. `kelvin` → `calendar.primary` + `calendar.others`; `mkkk` → `calendar.mkkk` + `calendar.mkkk-others`; staff resolves to `staff.schedules`). Callers can override with an explicit `calendars` array.

ai-doer is hard-coded as 24/7 working in the roster-adviser; for calendar-adviser, querying `person: 'ai-doer'` returns an empty events array (no calendar, not a failure).

Status: draft. Do not flip to active until calendar-adviser implementation ships and the boot self-check (Google OAuth + calendarList probe per §6.8 acceptance) passes once on `golden-ai-ops`.

contract_id.description: Contract identifier. Must be the literal string 'calendar.query.v1'.

trace_id.description: UUIDv7 generated at the caller's entry point. Propagated to the receiver's audit row.

dedupe_key.description: SHA256 of '<caller_agent_id>:calendar.query.v1:<person>:<window_canonical_json>:<calendars|empty>:<limit>' — allows the receiver to detect and short-circuit duplicate in-flight queries.

source_ref.description: URL or ID of the caller context that triggered this query (e.g. an ops.db Runs row id, a Handoff id, or a Slack thread ts). Used in the audit row for traceability back to the originating job.

caller_agent_id.description: Self-asserted agent ID of the caller (e.g. 'chief-of-staff', 'ai-doer'). Logged for legibility. Authorisation decisions key off SO_PEERCRED uid, not this value.

person.description: Whose calendar to query. Routes to the registry-declared default calendar set: kelvin → primary + others; mkkk → mkkk + mkkk-others; sally|chloe → staff.schedules. ai-doer → empty events (no calendar; lazy-resolves to roster-adviser at the consumer layer if presence is needed).

start.description: ISO 8601 datetime lower bound (inclusive). Events overlapping this instant or starting after it are returned.

end.description: ISO 8601 datetime upper bound (exclusive). Events ending at or before this instant are not included; events crossing the boundary are.

tz.description: IANA timezone (e.g. 'Europe/London', 'UTC'). The agent normalises returned event times to this zone before rendering.

window.description: Half-open time window for the query. Maximum span at v1: 31 days — wider windows are rejected with `bad_query` to bound cache reads.

calendars.description: Optional override of the per-person default calendar set. The five domain ids match registry/domains/. Callers SHOULD omit this and rely on the per-person routing unless they have a specific reason to narrow or widen the set (e.g. Phase 3's `## Constraints` only wants the primary calendar, not noisy 'others' events).

limit.description: Maximum number of events to return. The receiver returns the most-relevant slice (start-time ascending) and reports `truncated: true` if the limit was hit.
